destinations.evidence: The verifier enforces inclusive TTL bounds, rejects unsupported precompile metadata, and rejects zero executor addresses before accepting an async commitment as structurally valid.
After structural checks, the verifier validates and decodes the precompile payload and rejects executor or TTL drift between the payload and commitment fields.

equivalence_argument: The inline verifier function enforces TTL lower and upper bounds, supported metadata, nonzero executor, payload validity, and decoded executor/TTL parity. Unsupported async precompile addresses and registered-but-unported families are rejected through async_commitment_metadata and validate_async_payload.
notes: This is the same structure gate as 428 with medium-confidence atlas wording.
rationale: The added tests and proptests cover TTL lower and upper bounds, valid precompile membership for HTTP, LLM, long-running HTTP, ZK two-phase, and agent-call, invalid precompile rejection, and zero executor rejection. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async commitment structure validation must accept TTL values in the inclusive range 1..=MAX_TTL_BLOCKS, reject TTL 0 or values above MAX_TTL_BLOCKS, reject zero executor addresses, and accept only the configured async precompile set.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run validate_commitment_structure across TTL values 0, 1, MAX_TTL_BLOCKS, and MAX_TTL_BLOCKS+1, across every valid async precompile plus an arbitrary invalid address, and with zero versus nonzero executor addresses. Assert the accept/reject outcomes match the stated bounds and valid set.
summary: Async commitment structure constraints are enforced.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run validate_commitment_structure across TTL values 0, 1, MAX_TTL_BLOCKS, and MAX_TTL_BLOCKS+1, across every valid async precompile plus an arbitrary invalid address, and with zero versus nonzero executor addresses. Assert the accept/reject outcomes match the stated bounds and valid set.
title: Async commitment structure constraints
